parser.h(3) Library Functions Manual parser.h(3)

NAME

parser.h - Man page template parsing and section extraction.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t manpage_parser_parse_file (FILE *f, parsed_section_t **out_sections, size_t *out_count)
Parse man page sections from a FILE handle. asciichat_error_t manpage_parser_parse_memory (const char *content, size_t content_len, parsed_section_t **out_sections, size_t *out_count)
Parse man page sections from memory buffer. void manpage_parser_free_sections (parsed_section_t *sections, size_t count)
Free parsed sections array. const parsed_section_t * manpage_parser_find_section (const parsed_section_t *sections, size_t count, const char *section_name)
Find section by name.

Detailed Description

Man page template parsing and section extraction.

This module handles parsing man page templates to extract sections and metadata about their types (AUTO-generated, MANUAL, or MERGE sections).

Supports both FILE* and memory buffer parsing with automatic detection of platform capabilities.

Definition in file parser.h.

Function Documentation

const parsed_section_t * manpage_parser_find_section (const parsed_section_t * sections, size_t count, const char * section_name)

Find section by name. Searches the parsed sections array for a section with matching name. Comparison is case-sensitive.

Parameters

sections Array of parsed sections
count Number of sections
section_name Section name to search for (case-sensitive)

Returns

Pointer to matching section, or NULL if not found

Definition at line 396 of file parser.c.

397                                                                               {
398   if (!sections || !section_name) {
399     return NULL;
400   }
401 
402   for (size_t i = 0; i < count; i++) {
403     if (sections[i].section_name && strcmp(sections[i].section_name, section_name) == 0) {
404       return &sections[i];
405     }
406   }
407 
408   return NULL;
409 }




Referenced by find_section().






void
  manpage_parser_free_sections (parsed_section_t * sections, size_t
  count)


Free parsed sections array. Frees all allocated memory for
    sections array including section names and content. Safe to call with NULL
    pointers.




Parameters


sections Array of parsed sections (can be NULL)


count Number of sections in array


Definition at line 379 of file parser.c.


379                                                                             {
380   if (!sections) {
381     return;
382   }
383 
384   for (size_t i = 0; i < count; i++) {
385     if (sections[i].section_name) {
386       SAFE_FREE(sections[i].section_name);
387     }
388     if (sections[i].content) {
389       SAFE_FREE(sections[i].content);
390     }
391   }
392 
393   SAFE_FREE(sections);
394 }






References SAFE_FREE.


Referenced by free_parsed_sections().






asciichat_error_t manpage_parser_parse_file (FILE * f,
  parsed_section_t ** out_sections, size_t * out_count)


Parse man page sections from a FILE handle. Parses a man page
    template from an open FILE*, detecting:


    
  
  • Section headers (.SH directive)
    • 
  
  • Section markers (AUTO-START/END, MANUAL-START/END, MERGE-START/END)
    • 
  
  • Section content and boundaries
    • 



Memory is allocated for each section. Caller must use
    manpage_parser_free_sections() to clean up.




Parameters


f Open file handle to parse (cannot be NULL)


out_sections Pointer to array of parsed_section_t (output)


out_count Number of sections parsed (output)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success



ERROR_INVALID_PARAM if parameters are invalid




ERROR_CONFIG if parsing fails or memory allocation fails




Note


Caller must call
  manpage_parser_free_sections(out_sections, out_count)



The file position is not reset; caller may need to fseek if
  needed




Definition at line 338 of file parser.c.


338                                                                                                          {
339   if (!f || !out_sections || !out_count) {
340     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for manpage_parser_parse_file");
341   }
342 
343   return parse_sections_internal(f, out_sections, out_count);
344 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by parse_manpage_sections().






asciichat_error_t manpage_parser_parse_memory (const char
  * content, size_t content_len, parsed_section_t ** out_sections, size_t
  * out_count)


Parse man page sections from memory buffer. Parses man page
    content from a memory buffer, detecting sections and markers like
    parse_file() but from pre-loaded content.




Uses platform-specific optimizations:


    
  
  • Unix/macOS: fmemopen() for zero-copy parsing (if available)
    • 
  
  • Windows/fallback: Creates temporary file
    • 



Memory is allocated for each section. Caller must use
    manpage_parser_free_sections() to clean up.




Parameters


content Pointer to memory buffer containing man
  page (cannot be NULL)


content_len Length of buffer in bytes (must be > 0)


out_sections Pointer to array of parsed_section_t (output)


out_count Number of sections parsed (output)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success



ERROR_INVALID_PARAM if parameters are invalid




ERROR_CONFIG if parsing fails or memory allocation fails




Note


Caller must call
  manpage_parser_free_sections(out_sections, out_count)



Available in all builds (Debug, Dev, Release)




Definition at line 346 of file parser.c.


347                                                                  {
348   if (!content || content_len == 0 || !out_sections || !out_count) {
349     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for manpage_parser_parse_memory");
350   }
351 
352   // Create temporary file from memory
353   FILE *tmp = platform_tmpfile();
354   if (!tmp) {
355     return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to create temporary file for memory parsing");
356   }
357 
358   // Write content to temporary file
359   // Write content_len + 1 to include the null terminator.
360   // The embedded string is content_len + 1 bytes (content_len chars + null terminator).
361   // platform_getline() needs the null terminator to properly handle EOF.
362   size_t bytes_to_write = content_len + 1;
363   size_t written = fwrite(content, 1, bytes_to_write, tmp);
364   if (written != bytes_to_write) {
365     fclose(tmp);
366     return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to write complete content to temporary file");
367   }
368 
369   // Rewind to beginning for reading
370   rewind(tmp);
371 
372   // Parse using common function
373   asciichat_error_t err = parse_sections_internal(tmp, out_sections, out_count);
374   fclose(tmp);
375 
376   return err;
377 }






References ERROR_CONFIG, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    platform_tmpfile(), SET_ERRNO, and SET_ERRNO_SYS.
